title: Bulging Sensation - Pelvic Organ Prolapse
chief_complaint: 65-year-old G5P5 with sensation of vaginal bulge and pressure for 1 year, worsens with prolonged standing. Feels like something is falling out. No urinary symptoms.

physical_exam.pelvic: Stage 3 POP-Q: anterior vaginal wall prolapse (cystocele) to hymen +2cm, uterine descent to hymen (procidentia), small rectocele

correct_diagnosis: Stage 3 Pelvic Organ Prolapse (Cystocele + Uterine Prolapse)

patient_responses.bulge: I feel a lump coming down, especially when I stand

feedback.incorrect_message: This is pelvic organ prolapse. The POP-Q staging confirms it.
feedback.attending_note: Stage 3 POP: Offer pessary (ring or Gellhorn) as conservative option. If desires surgery: vaginal hysterectomy + anterior colporrhaphy. Sacrocolpopexy if younger and sexually active. Success rates >90%.
